title: HIGH: v220 header reads compression byte as half_float flag, then hardcodes ZIP
description: In src/Header.jl lines 68-72, for v220-221 the byte at the relevant position is labeled 'half_float flag' but is actually the compression flag byte. The code reads and discards it, then hardcodes UInt32(0x3) (ZIP + ACTIVE_MASK) at lines 84-88. For v220 files without ZIP compression, parsing will use wrong codec. Fix: use the actually-read byte to determine compression, not hardcode ZIP.
close_reason: Use actually-read compression byte instead of hardcoding ZIP. is_compressed_byte!=0 → ZIP, ==0 → NONE. ACTIVE_MASK always set for v220. 920 tests pass.

title: HIGH: read_bytes returns unsafe_wrap aliased memory - potential use-after-free
description: In src/Binary.jl lines 107-113, read_bytes returns a Vector{UInt8} created via unsafe_wrap that shares memory with the input bytes buffer. After GC.@preserve exits, the bytes pointer is no longer pinned. If bytes is garbage collected while the aliased vector is still in use, this is use-after-free. Currently safe because parse_vdb holds bytes for the entire parse, but fragile if calling patterns change. The docstring warns about this but callers in Compression.jl line 109 pass directly to decompressors. Fix: consider returning a copy for safety, or document the lifetime contract more prominently.
close_reason: Replaced unsafe_wrap aliased memory with safe byte slice copy. Eliminates use-after-free risk. 920 tests pass.

title: HIGH: Transforms.jl general affine fallback has undocumented 23-byte skip
description: In src/Transforms.jl lines 214-226, the fallback for unknown transform types reads a 4x4 matrix (128 bytes) + 6 extra doubles (48 bytes) + mysterious 23 bytes skip. The 23-byte skip is undocumented and likely wrong. OpenVDB C++ AffineMap::write() does NOT emit trailing padding. This was likely a guess that worked for one test file. Any VDB with a general AffineMap or NonlinearFrustumMap will parse wrong positions after the transform. Fix: verify against C++ reference for each map type and remove guesswork.
close_reason: Replaced untested general affine fallback (wrong 23-byte skip) with clear ArgumentError. All 20 test VDB files use only UniformScaleMap/UniformScaleTranslateMap/ScaleMap/ScaleTranslateMap. 920 tests pass.

title: Perf: prefix-sum popcount for O(1) count_on_before (100-500x speedup for get_value)
description: In src/Masks.jl lines 128-155, count_on_before iterates over complete UInt64 words with count_ones. For Internal2Mask (512 words), this is O(512) popcounts per get_value call - the dominant cost in tree traversal. Proposed fix: store a prefix-sum array alongside the mask. MaskWithPrefix{N,W} adds NTuple{W,Int32} where entry k = sum(popcount(words[1:k])). Then count_on_before becomes one table lookup + one masked popcount = O(1). Memory cost for I2: 512*4=2048 extra bytes per mask, negligible since I2 nodes are few (1-10 per grid). Expected speedup: 100-500x for get_value, transformative for ray marching inner loop.
close_reason: Added prefix-sum NTuple{W,UInt32} to Mask{N,W} for O(1) count_on_before and count_on

title: Add half-precision (Float16) support to Main Lyr parser
description:
TinyVDB correctly detects _HalfFloat grid type suffix and threads value_size=2 through the entire read pipeline. Main Lyr has no half-precision support — it hardcodes sizeof(T) everywhere.

Changes needed:
1. GridDescriptor.jl: Detect _HalfFloat suffix in grid type string, set half_precision flag, strip suffix for type parsing
2. Grid.jl: Compute value_size from half_precision flag, pass through read_tree

title: VDB integration test failure: bunny_cloud.vdb BoundsError in metadata parsing
description:
The bunny_cloud.vdb sample file fails to parse.

**Error:**
```
BoundsError: attempt to access 84066196-element Vector{UInt8} at index [188:1935764766]
...
read_string_with_size(bytes::Vector{UInt8}, pos::Int64)
read_grid_metadata(bytes::Vector{UInt8}, pos::Int64)
```

**Analysis:**
The error happens in `read_grid_metadata` calling `read_string_with_size`.
The index range `[188:1935764766]` suggests a huge length is read for a string, indicating we are reading garbage data as the size prefix.
This often happens if:
1. We misidentified the start of the grid.
2. The metadata format is different for this file version.
3. The previous section (Grid Descriptor) was parsed incorrectly.

**Next Steps:**
- Check the version of `bunny_cloud.vdb` (header version).
- Verify `read_grid_descriptor` offsets.
- Check if `read_file_metadata_v220` vs heuristic is applied correctly.

title: Perf: hierarchical DDA ray traversal for sparse volume rendering
description: Current ray marcher (Render.jl sphere_trace) does point-sampled sphere tracing. OpenVDB's production ray tracer uses hierarchical DDA that traverses tree structure directly, skipping empty space at each level: root level skips empty I2 regions (4096^3 voxels), I2 level skips empty I1 regions (128^3), I1 level skips empty leaf regions (8^3). This is O(active_leaves_along_ray) vs O(distance/step_size). Substantial engineering effort but orders-of-magnitude speedup for large sparse volumes.

title: Perf: ValueAccessor cache for O(1) repeated nearby lookups (5-8x for trilinear)
description: OpenVDB C++ uses ValueAccessor that caches most recently accessed leaf, I1, and I2 nodes. VDB access patterns are highly coherent - neighboring voxels share same leaf. Cache hit rate is 95%+ in practice. Lyr.jl has no accessor cache - every get_value does full root-to-leaf traversal. For trilinear interpolation (8 neighboring lookups), 7 of 8 hit same leaf yet all do full traversal. Proposed: mutable struct ValueAccessor{T} with cached_leaf, cached_i1, cached_i2. Check cached leaf origin first, fall back to full traversal. Expected speedup: 5-8x for trilinear, transformative for rendering (hundreds of sample_world calls per ray).
close_reason: Added ValueAccessor{T} with leaf/I1/I2 cache for O(1) repeated nearby lookups

title: VDB integration test failure: torus.vdb throws InexactError in read_compressed_bytes
description:
The torus.vdb sample file fails to parse during leaf value reading.

**Error:**
```
InexactError: convert(Int64, 0xe0c0808000000000)
...
read_compressed_bytes(bytes::Vector{UInt8}, pos::Int64, codec::ZipCodec, expected_size::Int64)
```

**Analysis:**
The parser reads a metadata byte of 6 (NO_MASK_AND_ALL_VALS) and then attempts to read compressed bytes.
The function `read_compressed_bytes` expects a `u64` size prefix.
The value read (`0xe0c0808000000000`) is huge and negative as an Int64, causing the error.
This suggests either:
1. There is no size prefix for this compression mode.
2. The size prefix is 32-bit.
3. The data is not compressed in the way we expect (Blosc chunk header might be different).

**Next Steps:**
- Investigate OpenVDB source for `readCompressedValues` or similar.
- Check if `ZipCodec` and `BloscCodec` should handle the size prefix differently.
- Verify if leaf data has a header when metadata=6.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design NanoVDB flat layout: header, node serialization, offset encoding
description: Design NanoVDB-inspired flat memory layout and document it in new src/NanoVDB.jl (included from Lyr.jl). Layout: single contiguous Vector{UInt8} containing (in order): fixed-size header (magic, grid count, value type tag, bbox, voxel_count, node counts, offsets to each section), then NanoRoot data, then NanoI2 nodes contiguously, then NanoI1 nodes, then NanoLeaf nodes. All inter-node references use byte offsets from buffer start (not pointers). Each node type fixed size so nodes indexed by ordinal. Define Julia structs for header and 'views' into each node type (hold buffer reference + offset, not copies). No tree building yet. Acceptance: all structs compile, sizeof(NanoLeafLayout) matches 512*sizeof(T)+64 bytes, layout documented with byte offsets.

title: NanoGrid: build complete flat buffer from VDB Tree

Algorithm: (1) traverse tree counting all I2, I1, leaf nodes; (2) allocate exact-size buffer; (3) write header with node counts and section offsets; (4) serialize root entries sorted by origin; (5) serialize I2 nodes recording byte offsets; (6) serialize I1 nodes; (7) serialize leaf nodes. Fill in child offset fields to point to correct byte positions. Acceptance: build_nanogrid succeeds on parsed cube.vdb, resulting NanoGrid has correct node counts, spot-checking leaf values matches original tree.

title: NanoI1/NanoI2: flat internal nodes with child offsets

I1: DIM=16, masks 512B each. I2: DIM=32, masks 4096B each. Methods: get_child_offset(node, child_idx)::UInt32, get_tile_value(node, tile_idx)::T. title: Fix v222 value section format - interleaved selection/values
description:
Investigation findings:

**Current understanding (WRONG):**
- values_start = grid_start_pos + block_offset
- Align to 16 bytes
- Read ALL selection masks (3152 × 64 = 201KB)
- Then read all raw Float32 values

**Actual format (based on byte analysis):**
- Valid SDF values are scattered throughout the values section
- Runs of 100-400 valid values with gaps between them
- Gaps likely contain selection masks or per-leaf metadata
- Format appears to be interleaved: [mask + values] repeated per leaf

**Evidence:**
- At values_start (490225): mostly garbage or zeros
- Valid SDFs found at offsets: 59-178, 1134-1340, 2189-2468, 3204-3595 (relative)
- These match expected per-leaf structure (512 voxels = 2048 bytes per leaf if dense)

**Fix needed:**
Change materialize_i2_values_v222 to:
1. For each leaf in topology order:
   a. Read selection mask (64 bytes)
   b. Read values for selected voxels
2. Or use read_dense_values() which properly handles the metadata+selection+compression format
close_reason: Partial fix: v222 parsing now produces 82% valid SDF values, 15% garbage. The format uses complex RLE-like selection mask encoding that needs more investigation. File parses without errors but values have some corruption. Filed follow-up issue needed for full fix.

title: Level set surface finding: DDA + per-voxel zero crossing
description: Implement find_surface(tree, ray, transform)->Union{SurfaceHit, Nothing} for level-set zero crossing. Algorithm: use VolumeRayIntersector to iterate leaves front-to-back. Within each leaf step through voxels via leaf-level DDA. Sample SDF value at each voxel. On sign change between consecutive samples, bisect between the two voxel centers (5-6 iterations for sub-voxel precision). Return SurfaceHit with t::Float64, position::SVec3d, normal::SVec3d (from SDF gradient), value::T. Replaces current sphere-tracing which misses thin features. Acceptance: on known SDF grid (sphere), hit position within 0.01 voxels of analytic answer; normals point outward.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 476e6c4: find_surface in src/Surface.jl — DDA voxel traversal + sign-change detection + bisection. 30 tests in test_surface.jl.

title: Complete v222 level set value format decoding
description:
The v222 level set format uses complex RLE-like selection mask encoding. Current implementation achieves 82% valid values, 15% garbage.

Findings from investigation:
- Selection masks are NOT 64 bytes of raw bits
- Format appears to be: [zeros][RLE mask bits][zeros][values]
- Leaf ordering in values section differs from topology order
- First leaf: 38 values starting at offset 27, not 188 at offset 64

To complete:
1. Study OpenVDB C++ source for exact v222 level set format
2. Implement proper selection mask decoding (possibly RLE)
3. Fix leaf ordering mismatch between topology and values
4. Target 100% valid values parsing

title: Implement DDA tree traversal for level set ray marching
description: Walk rays through the VDB tree leaf-by-leaf (like OpenVDB's VolumeRayIntersector) instead of blind sphere tracing. Current approach samples trilinear at arbitrary world points, which corrupts SDF at node boundaries (get_value returns background for empty space, trilinear blends real SDF with background). DDA traversal only samples within populated nodes, eliminating boundary artifacts entirely. Key ref: OpenVDB's tools/RayIntersector.h. Uses existing intersect_leaves() in Ray.jl as starting point. This is the correct production-quality fix.
close_reason: Duplicate of ck6p/9ysk — DDA tree traversal for level set ray marching fully implemented via VolumeRayIntersector + find_surface.

title: HIGH: read_active_values silently swallows BoundsError, pads with zero(T)
description: In src/Values.jl lines 179-194, a try/catch inside the loop catches BoundsError and replaces values with zero(T). This masks file corruption: truncated files get phantom zero-values instead of errors. For level sets, zero means 'on surface' creating phantom geometry. Additionally, zero(T) is not defined for NTuple{3,Float32}, so vector grids would throw MethodError. Fix: remove try/catch, let BoundsError propagate. Validate bounds before the loop if graceful handling needed.
close_reason: Removed try/catch that silently swallowed BoundsError and padded with zero(T). Truncated data now propagates as BoundsError. 920 tests pass.

title: Perf: remove overly conservative sphere trace step clamping
description: In src/Render.jl lines 122-123, step size is clamped to max vs*2.0. For Lipschitz-1 SDF, abs(dist) is already maximum safe step. Clamping to 2 voxels means that 100 voxels from surface, algorithm takes 50x more steps than necessary. Comment says this avoids 'trilinear corruption at node boundaries' - this is a workaround for a separate bug. Better solution: use nearest-neighbor sampling far from surface, trilinear only within few voxels. Expected speedup: 2-5x for large volumes.
close_reason: Already resolved: sphere_trace now delegates to find_surface (DDA + zero-crossing bisection). No step clamping remains.

title: CRITICAL: read_tile_value generic crashes for Bool/compound types - ltoh undefined
description: In src/Values.jl lines 156-163, the generic read_tile_value function applies ltoh() to the raw value. ltoh is only defined for primitive numeric types (UInt8/16/32/64, Int8/16/32/64, Float32, Float64). For compound types like NTuple{3,Float32} or Bool, ltoh will throw a MethodError at runtime. Currently masked by Float32/Float64 specializations at lines 166-172, but Bool grids (supported by parse_value_type in GridDescriptor.jl) will crash. Fix: add specializations for Int32, Int64, Bool, and NTuple types, or change generic to avoid ltoh for non-primitives.
close_reason: Added Int32, Int64, Bool specializations; generic fallback now errors instead of calling ltoh on unsupported types. 920 tests pass.

title: Perf: zero-allocation read_mask using ntuple instead of Vector intermediate
description: In src/Masks.jl lines 252-276, read_mask allocates Vector{UInt64}(undef, W) then converts to NTuple. For LeafMask (W=8, 64 bytes), this unnecessary allocation happens per leaf. Proposed: use ntuple(W) do i ... end to construct directly on stack. For W=8 (LeafMask), avoids heap allocation entirely. For W=512 (Internal2Mask), Julia will box anyway so benefit is limited to small masks. Expected improvement: measurable reduction in allocation count during parsing.
close_reason: Replaced Vector{UInt64} intermediate in read_mask with ntuple + direct pointer loads. Zero-allocation mask reading.
